title: podman failing in Travis CI
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: **kind bug**

**Description**

I've started to look into the possibility of adding podman Travis CI configuration to https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container, besides the existing docker. The attempt is in branch https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/tree/podman.

I configure Travis to use Xenial, I use `ppa:projectatomic/ppa`.

When I only install `podman`, I get ((https://travis-ci.org/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/471131847)

```
STEP 4: RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy]: : exec: "runc": executable file not found in $PATH
```

so it looks like the Ubuntu `podman` package lacks dependency on `runc`. When I explicitly also install `runc`, I get (https://travis-ci.org/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/471450579)

```
STEP 4: RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy]: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string
: exit status 1
error building at step {Env:[DISTTAG=f29container FGC=f29 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy Original:RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy}: error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have GitHub repository with working Travis CI setup that uses `docker`. Try to add podman-based tests.
2. Add `before_install` step `if test "$docker" == "sudo podman" ; then sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa ; sudo apt-get update -y ; sudo apt-get install -y podman ; fi` and in `script`, call `sudo podman ...` instead of `docker ...`

**Describe the results you received:**

Without explicitly adding `runc`:
```
STEP 4: RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy]: : exec: "runc": executable file not found in $PATH
```

After adding `runc`:
```
STEP 4: RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy]: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string
: exit status 1
error building at step {Env:[DISTTAG=f29container FGC=f29 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy Original:RUN groupadd -g 288 kdcproxy ; useradd -u 288 -g 288 -c 'IPA KDC Proxy User' -d '/var/lib/kdcproxy' -s '/sbin/nologin' kdcproxy}: error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The package `podman` in https://launchpad.net/~projectatomic/+archive/ubuntu/ppa should work out of box in Travis CI Xenial setup.

**Output of `podman version`:**

No idea as I don't have interactive console on Travis CI, but Travis log shows
```
$ if test "$docker" == "sudo podman" ; then sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa ; sudo apt-get update -y ; sudo apt-get install -y podman runc ; fi
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

No idea.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Travis CI

comments.body: We pulled our local Travis CI because the kernels they provide are rather ancient, so I imagine that's potentially a culprit here. Are you using the containerized Travis tests, or bare-metal VM ones?

I use the following https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/blob/podman/.travis.yml. I have

```
language: generic
dist: xenial
sudo: required
```

Which I believe is Ubuntu 16.* VM environment.

In any case, neither the `runc` dependency, nor the error about `json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string` seem like a kernel issue.

The `runc` problem is interesting, because `cri-o-runc` is being installed. Podman might not be looking in the right path for it. This could also be the cause of the second issue in the runtime - we bundle it in the PPA because the Ubuntu repos have a very old version.

@adelton Can you add `--log-level=debug` to the build command, to see what runtime path it's selecting?

@TomSweeneyRedHat I'm looking at the code for `podman build` and it doesn't seem like it respects OCI runtime path from `libpod`, which would explain this - the runtime path is properly set to use the CRI-O runc binary in `libpod.conf`, but `podman build` isn't respecting this
I think we should change main.go to figure out the runc to use and then all tools would follow that rule.

Interesting, it seems that with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2059 merged, I no longer see the `exec: "runc": executable file not found in $PATH` error. In fact, when I remove the `runc` from the `apt-get install` list, I even no longer get the `json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string`: https://travis-ci.org/adelton/freeipa-container/builds/474853076

So it has improved the situation quite a bit ... but it looks like there is still that `json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string` issue even with latest podman/libpod when `runc` happens to be installed.

But it no longer blocks testing with podman on Travis CI -- I simply don't install `runc` with `podman` to avoid the `json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string`. So closing this general Travis CI use-case issue.
I think we probably want to change the PPA to have our runc conflict with the distro's runc
